warnings: Species Turbinaria reniformis  and Styl phora pistillata were not included in the paper, but were included in the results
Sediment levels were converted from mg/l to mg/cm2/day and UoM, but UoM could not be derived from the text for sediment level in 2 setups
Maximum quantum yield is reported in 4 setups, but only the last 2 values are given as a range
Photosynthesis and respiration are not reported in 2 setups
Sediment exposure duration is not reported in the paper, only mentioned as ‘2h’
The following setups do not have a matching response object: 0, 5, 8, 11, 14, 17, 20
num_responses=10 but setup response counts are [3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3, 3]

bibliography.title: Influence of salinity and sedimentation on Vibrio infection of the Hawaiian coral Montipora capitata

exposure.sediment_composition: Top 5 cm of sediment from the same fringing reef
exposure.notes: sediments were drained of excess seawater, dried for 48 h at 60°C, and then aliquoted into glass vials (with the appropriate mass of sediment for each coral fragment) before sterilization via autoclaving. Sterilization was conducted to remove any potential pathogens that may have been present in reef sediments.

warnings: figure [11 x 11] intentionally omitted. Please see paper text.
